name: Site Suitability Analysis for Solar Farms
description: Identify suitable locations for solar farm development by excluding unsuitable zones (water, forest, steep terrain, urban) and prioritizing accessible areas with good solar potential.
author: CortexGIS

aoi.description: Area of Interest as a polygon

steps.description: Get Digital Elevation Model for slope analysis
Compute Euclidean distance to nearest road
Keep areas within 5 km of roads (accessibility constraint)
Overlay all constraint masks to get candidate areas
Rank suitable cells by solar potential (irradiance)
Find contiguous clusters of suitable land (>10 ha minimum)
Convert raster clusters to vector polygons
Calculate statistics for each site (area, mean elevation, mean slope, mean solar potential)

cot_reasoning: 1. User wants to identify suitable areas for solar farm development.
2. Define constraints: exclude water/forest/urban/wetlands; keep slope <5°; access roads.
3. Fetch landcover map to identify unsuitable zones (water, forest, urban).
4. Fetch DEM and compute slope; exclude areas >5° (difficult to construct, higher costs).
5. Download OSM roads to measure accessibility; buffer 1-5 km (balances cost and connection).
6. Fetch solar irradiance data (MERRA-2 or similar) to rank suitable sites.
7. Combine all constraint layers using logical AND: only areas passing ALL criteria.
8. Rank resulting areas by solar irradiance to prioritize high-potential sites.
9. Identify contiguous clusters (>10 hectares minimum viable farm size).
10. Vectorize clusters and compute final attributes (area, elevation, solar, distance-to-roads).
11. Generate list of ranked candidate sites for stakeholder review.

validation_metrics.expected_output_type: raster map + vector polygons + summary table

caveats: Results are based on average environmental data; local factors (soil quality, environmental permits, land ownership) not included.
Solar irradiance data is modeled; actual production depends on weather, panel orientation, system design.
Road distance threshold (1-5 km) is arbitrary; adjust based on grid capacity and transmission costs.
Cluster minimum size (10 ha) assumes medium-scale farms; adjust for different project scales.
next_steps: Ground-truth validation on selected candidate sites
Land ownership and legal feasibility check
references: Charabi & Gastli (2011) - GIS for Solar Site Selection
